findings.evidence: grep hit `Basic.lean:195:lemma hasDerivAt_iteratedDerivWithin_succ`; proof calls `hcm.contDiffOn.differentiableOn_iteratedDerivWithin` and never uses `neg_one_pow_mul_iteratedDerivWithin_nonneg`.
findings.fix: State the general smoothness lemma first, e.g. from `ContDiffOn ℝ (k+1) f (Ici 0)` or at least `ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ f (Ici 0)`, and derive the CM method as a wrapper if desired.
findings.issue: `IsCompletelyMonotone.hasDerivAt_iteratedDerivWithin_succ` assumes complete monotonicity, but the proof only uses `hcm.contDiffOn`; the sign condition is unused.

findings.evidence: grep hit `Integral.lean:57:lemma iteratedDerivWithin_Icc_eq_Ici`; proof uses `hf.contDiffOn.contDiffAt ...` only to get `hcda`, then rewrites both sides with `iteratedDerivWithin_eq_iteratedDeriv`.
findings.fix: Generalize to a lemma with `ContDiffAt ℝ (n : WithTop ℕ∞) f t` (or an appropriate `ContDiffOn` hypothesis) and use it inside the CM Taylor-sign lemma.
findings.issue: `IsCompletelyMonotone.iteratedDerivWithin_Icc_eq_Ici` assumes complete monotonicity, but the equality only needs local smoothness at `t` plus the set memberships/unique-differentiability facts.

findings.evidence: grep hit `BernsteinMeasures.lean:73:lemma continuousOn_chafaiDensity`; proof only uses `hcm.contDiffOn.continuousOn_iteratedDerivWithin` after unfolding the density.
findings.fix: Change the hypothesis to `ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ f (Ici 0)` or the finite differentiability level needed for `n`, and pass `hcm.contDiffOn` at CM call sites.
findings.issue: `continuousOn_chafaiDensity` assumes `IsCompletelyMonotone f`, but continuity of the density only uses smoothness of `f`; nonnegativity/sign alternation is irrelevant.

summary: Several public lemmas are stated as completely-monotone facts even though their proofs use only smoothness, making the API less reusable than the natural Mathlib level.
